content:
First note that

 p(5)=125a+25b+5c+d,  
 p(25)=15625a+625b+25c+d,

so  
 p(5)+p(25)=(125+15625)a+(25+625)b+(5+25)c+2d  
    =15750a+650b+30c+2d=1906.  

Divide by 2:

 7875a+325b+15c+d=953,  

so

 d=953−7875a−325b−15c.  

Hence

 p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d  
  =3375a+225b+15c+(953−7875a−325b−15c)  
  =953−4500a−100b.  

Set B=b+45a.  Then p(15)=953−100B.  As B runs over the integers, p(15) takes the values

 …,,953−800=153,\;953−900=53,\;953−1000=−47,\;953−1100=−147,\dots

The choice B=10 gives p(15)=953−1000=−47, whose absolute value 47 is the smallest possible.  For instance, one can take

 a=1,\quad b=10−45=−35,\quad c=0,\quad d=953−7875·1−325·(−35)=4453,

which indeed yields p(5)+p(25)=1906 and p(15)=−47.  Hence

Answer: ⎡47⎤.

First note that

 p(5)=125a+25b+5c+d,  
 p(25)=15625a+625b+25c+d,

so  
 p(5)+p(25)=(125+15625)a+(25+625)b+(5+25)c+2d  
    =15750a+650b+30c+2d=1906.  

Divide by 2:

 7875a+325b+15c+d=953,  

so

 d=953−7875a−325b−15c.  

Hence

 p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d  
  =3375a+225b+15c+(953−7875a−325b−15c)  
  =953−4500a−100b.  

Set B=b+45a.  Then p(15)=953−100B.  As B runs over the integers, p(15) takes the values

 …,,953−800=153,\;953−900=53,\;953−1000=−47,\;953−1100=−147,\dots

The choice B=10 gives p(15)=953−1000=−47, whose absolute value 47 is the smallest possible.  For instance, one can take

 a=1,\quad b=10−45=−35,\quad c=0,\quad d=953−7875·1−325·(−35)=4453,

which indeed yields p(5)+p(25)=1906 and p(15)=−47.  Hence

Answer: ⎡47⎤.
